subject: Project Management
title: Therac-25
description: A fast breakdown of Therac-25 for builders.

hook.text: Therac-25 was a radiation therapy machine. Between 1985 and 1987, it killed three patients.

buildup.text: A software bug allowed the machine to deliver radiation doses 100 times stronger than intended.

discovery.text: Earlier models had hardware interlocks as backup. Therac-25 removed them — trusting software alone.

twist.text: When patients reported burning pain, operators blamed them. The machine's logs showed 'no malfunction.'

climax.text: Therac-25 became the defining case study for why software in critical systems needs independent safety checks.

punchline.text: The machine said it was fine. The patients said otherwise.

quiz.question: What made Therac-25 dangerous compared to earlier models?
options: Hardware safety interlocks were removed, trusting software alone
It used a stronger radiation source
